message:
feat(string): port consttime_memequal constant-time byte equality (bd-gtwx7)

Adds NetBSD's `consttime_memequal(b1, b2, len)` — returns 1 if the
first `len` bytes of `b1` and `b2` are byte-equal, 0 otherwise.
Always touches every byte regardless of where the inputs differ;
used by crypto code (TLS / SSH MAC verification) to compare
hashes without timing leaks.

`len == 0` always returns 1 (NetBSD convention: empty buffers are
trivially equal).

Implementation: thin abi shim that delegates to the existing
constant-time `timingsafe_bcmp` and inverts the result.
`timingsafe_bcmp` returns 0 = equal / non-zero = not-equal; this
wrapper flips that to 1 = equal / 0 = not-equal to match the
NetBSD return convention. NULL handling inherits from
`timingsafe_bcmp` (same pointer → equal, mixed NULL/non-NULL →
not-equal).

Tests: 7 abi integration tests covering equal-buffers-return-1,
single-byte mismatches at start / middle / end positions,
zero-length, NULL-pointer-handling-inherited-from-bcmp, and a
1024-byte buffer test that exercises the full-scan property.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-gtf.5","title":"L2 partial-replace battery + standalone dependency policy gate","description":"Background:
- L2 requires a subset standalone path without hidden host-libc dependencies.

Scope:
- Define L2 program battery and verify bootstrap/threading/NSS/locale/iconv prerequisites for declared subset.
- Add explicit exclusion list for out-of-scope dynamic features.

Deliverables:
1) L2 subset battery specification.
2) Dependency/policy checks for standalone claims.
3) L2 pass/fail artifact template.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Declared L2 subset runs deterministically without forbidden dependencies.
- Exclusions are explicit and test-verified.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for standalone policy checks.
- E2E L2 subset program runs.
- Logs: trace_id, level=L2, program, dependency_scan, outcome, exclusion_reason.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.853753773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:54:35.454885868Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:54:35.454604295Z","close_reason":"Deliverables in working tree from Codex swarm.  {"id":"bd-gtf.6","title":"L3 full-replace claim-control gate tied to prerequisite completion","description":"Background:
- L3 full replacement claim has the highest compatibility and risk burden.

Scope:
- Define L3 dynamic-program battery and claim-control gate requiring completion of hard-parts, symbol coverage, and no-forbidden call-through conditions.
- Enforce claim immutability unless evidence set is complete.

Deliverables:
1) L3 claim gate specification.
2) Dynamic-program battery requirements.
3) Claim-control policy integrated into release process.

Acceptance Criteria:
- L3 claim blocked unless all prerequisite gates are satisfied.
- Any drift after claim invalidates release readiness.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for claim-control logic.
- E2E L3 candidate runs on dynamic programs.
- Logs: trace_id, level=L3, prerequisite_status, claim_decision, blocking_reasons.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

Verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_dossier_validator_test and rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_dossier_validator_test -- -D warnings; workspace cargo check passed, while workspace fmt/clippy still fail in unrelated frankenlibc-bench files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["l3","policy","replacement"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gtf.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1j4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gtf.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gtf.6","depends_on_id":"bd-gtf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gtf.6","depends_on_id":"bd-gtf.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-gtid","title":"bd-1gh subtask: Strict+hardened verification pack and stub-retirement gate","description":"Background:
- Stub elimination requires evidence that pthread_key APIs are production-usable under real threading workloads.

Goal:
- Build strict/hardened conformance+e2e verification pack for pthread_key APIs and retire stub status in target scope.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture and integration scenario set for key APIs.
2) Support matrix/status updates with explicit semantics.
3) Gate hooks to prevent regression to stub/callthrough behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests pass in both modes with deterministic outputs.
- Stub/callthrough regressions fail guards.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit + e2e scripts with detailed structured logs and artifact links.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.703424496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:46:41.647974596Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:46:41.647906559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","pthread","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1092,"issue_id":"bd-gtid","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"PurpleHawk: Created conformance fixture pack tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_tls_keys.json with 16 cases covering all four TLS APIs (key_create, key_delete, getspecific, setspecific) plus destructor semantics. Covers: success paths, EAGAIN exhaustion, EINVAL for invalid/deleted/OOB keys, default zero value, roundtrip, destructor iteration and bounds, null-value skip. All 34 unit tests pass for both strict and hardened semantics.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:46:41Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-gtwx7","title":"PORT: consttime_memequal (NetBSD constant-time byte equality)","description":"Port the NetBSD consttime_memequal(b1, b2, len) constant-time byte equality test. Returns 1 if the first len bytes of b1 and b2 are byte-equal, 0 otherwise. Always touches every byte regardless of where they differ. Used by crypto code (TLS, SSH MAC verification) to compare hashes/MACs without timing leaks. Implementation: thin abi shim that delegates to the existing core::string::timingsafe::bcmp helper and inverts the result (timingsafe_bcmp returns 0=equal, non-zero=not-equal; consttime_memequal flips that to 1=equal, 0=not-equal). Tests: equal buffers return 1, single-byte mismatch returns 0, zero-length always returns 1, NULL handling matches existing timingsafe_bcmp semantics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:29:16.281702048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:29:16.281702048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gwqja","title":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread trampoline spins unbounded","description":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread start trampoline spins without a bound while waiting for handoff

The host-thread bootstrap path contains an unbounded busy-wait:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1390-1399` loops on `start_ctx.host_thread.load(Ordering::Acquire)` until the parent stores a non-zero thread id.
- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.278149696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.276052826Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}

 {"id":"bd-h2h5","title":"syscall: mount_setattr NULL pathname should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0, NULL, 0). Tighten the ABI regression from a broad errno family to an exact host-parity assertion for the null-path fast path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:35.038153706Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.706311440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.702833586Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity mount_setattr(NULL) behavior: exact EINVAL instead of a broad errno family; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-mjon","title":"bd-yos subtask: Thread bootstrap trampoline finalization for pthread_create","description":"Background:
- Thread bootstrap is a core blocker for both callthrough removal and TLS key APIs.

Goal:
- Finalize clone/trampoline startup path for pthread_create with explicit invariants and ABI boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Thread start trampoline contract (entry args, return handling, cleanup path).
2) Parent/child synchronization at startup.
3) Error path behavior and errno propagation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pthread_create success/failure semantics match declared support matrix behavior.
- No hidden host-libc dependency in startup path for covered mode/profile.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for bootstrap components and error branches.
- Structured logs: trace_id, thread_id, parent_id, start_state, exit_state, errno, timing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonForge","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.822186049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139407950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139340033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":1123,"issue_id":"bd-mjon","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Thread bootstrap trampoline implementation.

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs: Added clone_thread_asm (inline asm trampoline for Linux clone syscall)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added SYS_GETTID, SYS_SET_TID_ADDRESS, sys_gettid, sys_exit_thread, sys_clone_thread
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: Complete native thread creation via clone (ThreadHandle, create_thread, join_thread, detach_thread, self_tid)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mod.rs: Updated re-exports

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core: PASS
- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::thread: 6/6 PASS

Tests:
- create_and_join_thread_returns_value
- child_thread_can_write_shared_memory
- multiple_threads_created_and_joined
- detach_thread_does_not_error
- join_null_handle_returns_einval
- gettid_returns_positive

Architecture:
- Uses CLONE_VM|CLONE_FS|CLONE_FILES|CLONE_SIGHAND|CLONE_THREAD|CLONE_SYSVSEM|CLONE_SETTLS|CLONE_PARENT_SETTID|CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID
- Child stack: [fn_ptr][arg] layout, trampoline pops both, calls fn_ptr(arg), SYS_exit on return
- Join uses futex-wait on TID (kernel clears via CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID and wakes waiters)
- Parent/child sync via startup futex (ensures args consumed before create returns)
- 2MiB stack with 4KiB guard page via mmap","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mk07a","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc ctype.h diff matrix","description":"Differential conformance harness shipped in commit 631b5100 (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_ctype.rs).

Coverage:
- 12 classifiers (isalpha/isdigit/isalnum/isspace/isupper/islower/isprint/ispunct/isxdigit/isblank/iscntrl/isgraph)
- 2 transformers (toupper/tolower)
- Sweep: EOF + every byte 0..=255 (257 inputs)
- 3598 differential calls vs glibc reference
- 0 divergences observed

Result: ctype.h family is conformant against glibc in the C/POSIX locale.

Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_conformance_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test conformance_diff_ctype","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped in commit 631b5100; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
 {"id":"bd-mkf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc mkfifo+mknod diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: mkfifo (S_IFIFO mode round-trip via stat + EEXIST edge), mkfifoat (AT_FDCWD), mknod (S_IFREG + S_IFIFO modes), mknodat (AT_FDCWD). Char/block device creation excluded (CAP_MKNOD required). 7 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","mkfifo","mknod"]}
-{"id":"bd-mll8f","title":"PORT: raise_default_signal (NetBSD libutil graceful-shutdown helper)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil raise_default_signal(sig) helper. It saves the current sigaction for sig, installs SIG_DFL with the same mask cleared, raises the signal (which terminates the process if the default action is core/term), then if control returns restores the prior sigaction and unblocks. Used by graceful-shutdown code paths so that SIGTERM/SIGINT propagate the correct exit status from the parent shell. Signature: int raise_default_signal(int sig). Returns 0 on success, -1 with errno on failure. Implementation: thin abi shim using existing sigaction/sigprocmask wrappers; tests cover SIG_USR1 round trip with a custom handler that flags pre/post invocation, NULL-handler default, error path on bad signum.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:21:05.903676765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:21:05.903676765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-mll8f","title":"PORT: raise_default_signal (NetBSD libutil graceful-shutdown helper)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil raise_default_signal(sig) helper. It saves the current sigaction for sig, installs SIG_DFL with the same mask cleared, raises the signal (which terminates the process if the default action is core/term), then if control returns restores the prior sigaction and unblocks. Used by graceful-shutdown code paths so that SIGTERM/SIGINT propagate the correct exit status from the parent shell. Signature: int raise_default_signal(int sig). Returns 0 on success, -1 with errno on failure. Implementation: thin abi shim using existing sigaction/sigprocmask wrappers; tests cover SIG_USR1 round trip with a custom handler that flags pre/post invocation, NULL-handler default, error path on bad signum.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:21:05.903676765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:25:32.822808014Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:25:32.820829778Z","close_reason":"shipped: raise_default_signal with block-swap-raise-restore atomic sequence + 3 abi tests covering SIGURG round-trip, invalid signum, null signal, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mn-1","title":"PORT: mntent sub-bead 1 — core::mntent pure-safe parser/serializer","description":"Add crates/frankenlibc-core/src/mntent/mod.rs with: MntFields<a> (six byte-slice field references), parse_mntent_line(line) -> Option<MntFields> handling blank/comment lines, freq/passno default-zero parsing, has_mnt_opt(opts, opt) -> Option<usize> scanning comma-separated tokens, and format_mntent_line(fields, out: &mut Vec<u8>) producing fstab-style "fsname dir type opts freq passno
". All operate on &[u8]; no allocation in parsers; tests cover blank/comment skip, partial fields, leading/trailing whitespace, options with embedded commas as separators, edge-case option matching at start/end of string.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:27:47.180840Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:29:33.308150Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:29:33.308150Z","close_reason":"Added crates/frankenlibc-core/src/mntent/{mod.rs,parse.rs} with: borrowed MntFields<a> (six byte-slice field references), parse_mntent_line() (handles blank/comment lines, EOL stripping, runs of whitespace, default-zero freq/passno fallback), has_mnt_opt() (token-aware comma-separated option scan), parse_mntent_freq_passno() (i32 with garbage-fallback), and format_mntent_line() (in-place i32-to-decimal serialization including i32::MIN). 26 unit tests cover blank/comment skip, partial fields, leading whitespace, run-collapse, embedded-token boundary cases (substring guard), key=value option matching, round-trip identity, and a real /proc/mounts line.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mn-2","title":"PORT: mntent sub-bead 2 — rewire abi shims to core","description":"Refactor unistd_abi.rs hasmntopt, getmntent_r, addmntent to delegate to core::mntent::{has_mnt_opt, parse_mntent_line, format_mntent_line}. Keep the C-ABI marshalling (mntent struct field pointers, caller buffer packing, BufReader/Write integration) at the boundary. Verify any conformance_diff_mntent tests still pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:27:47.180840Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","close_reason":"Refactored frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs hasmntopt (was ~12 lines of inline window/comma scan), getmntent_r (was ~85 lines of inline parser + 4x copy_nonoverlapping NUL packing), and addmntent (was ~15 lines of CStr->String->format!). All three now delegate to frankenlibc_core::mntent::{has_mnt_opt, parse_mntent_line, format_mntent_line}. The C-ABI marshalling — mntent struct field-pointer writes, caller-buffer NUL packing via a closure, BufReader/Write integration — stays at the boundary.  {"id":"bd-mn-epic","title":"PORT: mntent (fstab/mtab) family native Rust impl","description":"Move getmntent_r line-parsing, hasmntopt option scan, and addmntent line formatting from frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (~300 lines of pointer arithmetic + manual NUL packing) into a pure-safe core::mntent module operating on byte slices.
